data.title: Chip Maker TSMC Is More Bullish Than Ever on AI, Despite Iran War — WSJ
data.description:
By Yang Jie in Tokyo and Sherry Qin in Singapore

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing signaled confidence in global demand for artificial intelligence despite the war in Iran, raising its forecast for revenue and playing down the risk of supply-chain disruptions.

TSMC, a contract chip maker for clients such as Nvidia and Apple, projected that its capital expenditures for the year would hit the higher end of the $52 billion to $56 billion range projected in January. The company said it expected revenue to grow more than 30% this year in dollar terms, from an earlier estimate of around 30%.

TSMC's chief executive, C.C. Wei, said the company had double-checked with customers about AI demand and they reassured the chip maker that it was still strong. That led TSMC to accelerate investments such as building more clean rooms, he said.

The company reported a gross profit margin of 66% for the first quarter, the highest level in more than 20 years, according to data from S&P Global Market Intelligence, despite weakness in some segments, including an 11% decline in its smartphone business compared with the previous quarter.

"For anyone wondering whether the AI trade still has legs, TSMC just told us that business has never been better," Josh Gilbert, an analyst at eToro, said in a note.

TSMC's aggressive capital spending and lofty profit margins have made it the nerve center of the global semiconductor industry. Equipment and material makers are clustering around its expanding hubs in Taiwan, the U.S. and beyond. They are aligning with TSMC's road map to get their share of the profit, further cementing TSMC's leadership in the global chip supply chain.

Taiwan's benchmark stock index has notched record highs in the past few sessions, including on Thursday when it was up 1.1%. TSMC's shares closed at a new high Thursday, giving the company a market capitalization of $1.7 trillion, after an initial drop following the outbreak of the Iran war in late February.

Taiwan has other AI-related suppliers such as Foxconn, which builds Nvidia's servers, and companies that specialize in the technology to stack and integrate chip components, an essential element in building AI processors.

The Middle East conflict has done little to damp the optimism, but it still poses risks. Taiwan relies heavily on imported fuel for electricity, and chip production is power-intensive.

Last week, TSMC Vice President Cliff Hou called on Taiwan to increase strategic reserves and diversify procurement channels of helium, hydrogen and natural gas to ensure a stable supply.

TSMC executives said Thursday that with Taiwan securing sufficient liquefied natural gas supplies through at least May, the company doesn't expect near-term disruptions to production. They said specialty gases such as helium and hydrogen were sourced from multiple suppliers and regions, and any issues were unlikely to have a material impact on chip output.

Qatar supplies a chunk of the world's helium, which is needed for cooling in chip production.

TSMC's Wei said the company has acquired additional land in Arizona, its U.S. production center, to expand capacity and meet long-term demand from leading U.S. customers.

Write to Yang Jie at jie.yang@wsj.com

data.title: SEATech Ventures 2025 10-K: $0 Revenue, $(0.00) EPS, Net Loss $(39,341)
data.description:
SEATech Ventures reported no revenue for the years ended December 31, 2025 and 2024 and posted a net loss of $39,341 for 2025 (net loss per share – basic and diluted: $0.00), reflecting reduced activity and restructuring. The company recorded no gross profit and operating losses reflected largely in general and administrative expenses. Management is pursuing cost reductions and advisory-driven fee opportunities to generate near-term revenue.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 for the years ended Dec 31, 2025 and 2024.
  • Gross Profit: $0; no cost of revenue incurred and no gross profit for the years ended Dec 31, 2025 and 2024.
  • Operating Income: Not explicitly presented; operating loss reflected in general and administrative expenses of $97,302 for 2025.
  • Net Income: Net loss of $(39,341) for the year ended Dec 31, 2025 (compared to $(156,926) in 2024).
  • Net Income Per Share: Net loss per share – basic and diluted: $0.00 for the year ended Dec 31, 2025.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and focus: Company reported no revenue in 2024–2025 and has shifted focus to advisory and mentorship services rather than direct sales.
  • Corporate structure and geographic focus: Reorganized into a Labuan holding entity with a Hong Kong operating hub and expanded emphasis on Hong Kong and Southeast Asia operations.
  • Service and brand pivot: Transitioned toward an ICT Start-Up Mentorship Program and corporate advisory/family office services to build fee-based client traction.
  • Operational changes: Divested subsidiaries and other investments during 2025 and reduced operating headcount and general and administrative costs following director resignations.
  • Outlook: Management plans to pursue private placements, further cost reductions, and leverage advisory expansion to generate immediate fee revenue.

Original SEC Filing: SEATech Ventures Corp. [ SEAV ] - 10-K - Apr. 16, 2026

data.title: Brand Engagement Network 10-K: $0.28M Revenue, $(1.97) EPS
data.description:
Brand Engagement Network reported immaterial revenue of $0.275 million for the year, with net loss of $8.625 million, or $(1.97) per share, reflecting reduced losses versus the prior year as operating expenses and one-time charges declined. The company remains development-stage with limited commercial sales beyond pilots while continuing to invest in product development and capitalized software.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0.275 million (increased from $0.099 million in prior year)
  • Operating Income: $(12.626) million (loss from operations improved $23.848 million vs prior year)
  • Net Income: $(8.625) million (net loss reduced by $25.090 million vs prior year)
  • Net Income Per Share: $(1.97) per basic and diluted share

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Trajectory: Reported immaterial revenues with focus on development-stage activity; commercial sales remain minimal beyond pilot engagements.
  • Product & IP Positioning: Developing a security-focused multimodal conversational AI with proprietary NLP, multisensory and personalization intellectual property targeting automotive, healthcare and finance sectors.
  • Operational Shift: Reduction in operating expenses and one-time charges in 2025 improved operating performance and streamlined general and administrative costs following prior-year transaction costs.
  • Go-to-Market & Partnerships: Pursuing commercialization in Latin America via a licensing arrangement with Skye and targeting automotive and healthcare channels for deployment.
  • R&D & Scaling: Continuing research and development, capitalizing software and placing technology into service while planning to scale commercial and sales capabilities.

Original SEC Filing: Brand Engagement Network Inc. [ BNAI ] - 10-K - Apr. 16, 2026

data.title: Euro zone yields dip on Middle East peace hopes, but are well above pre-war levels
data.description:
By Alun John

Euro zone government bond yields nudged lower on Thursday, and traders further reduced bets on an imminent ECB rate hike, as optimism grew that the war in the Middle East may be near an end.

Unlike world stocks, however, bonds remain well off pre-war levels.

Germany's 10-year bond yield, the euro zone benchmark, dropped 2 basis points to 3.03%, (DE10YT=RR) moving further from late March's high of 3.13%.

It is, however, still much closer to its highs than pre-war levels around 2.7%, as traders anticipate the energy price surge caused by the war, and its potential for spilling over into broader inflation, will still force the European Central Bank to raise interest rates.

Markets are currently pricing two, 25-basis point ECB rate hikes this year, down from around three a few weeks ago. Before the war, however, traders saw some chance of an ECB rate cut in 2026. (0#EURIRPR)

They are pricing just a 20% chance of a rate hike at the ECB's meeting this month.

The optimism towards bonds, at least compared with late March, was helped by news a key Pakistani mediator was in Tehran and U.S. President Donald Trump's administration was talking up hopes for a deal that would open the crucial Strait of Hormuz.

Israel's cabinet also met on Wednesday to discuss a possible ceasefire in neighbouring Lebanon, a senior Israeli official said.

Goldman Sachs on Thursday pushed back their expectations for ECB hikes - they see two rate hikes this year, but in June and September as opposed to April and June as they had previously thought.

"The incoming information has reduced the urgency for ECB rate hikes. Energy prices have fallen from their peak following last week’s ceasefire, recent price-related indicators have not moved up decisively and recent ECB communication has shown little urgency for a near-term policy change," Goldman said.

They also said that they do not anticipate their expected two rate hikes for 2026 will be sustained and they see the ECB cutting its key rate back to its current 2% in 2027.

Influential ECB board member Isabel Schnabel said on Wednesday the ECB can "afford to take the time that is needed to analyse the character of this shock".

"We do not need to rush into action," she said.

Euro zone inflation data for March, published on Thursday, showed a 2.6% rise year on year, slightly above the 2.5% in initial data, but not sufficiently higher to cause a dramatic reassessment by the ECB.

Rate-sensitive two-year yields fell more than their longer-dated peers, and Germany's two-year yield dropped 3 basis points to 2.51%. (DE2YT=RR)

That in turn pushed the gap between Germany's two-year and 10-year yields to 51 basis points, the most, and so the steepest yield curve, in a month. (DE2DE10=RR)

Italian debt, which has been underperforming the German benchmark when yields were rising, outperformed on Thursday. Its 10-year yield dropped 3 bps to 3.79%.

data.title: Berenberg lowers Antofagasta to 'hold', sees long-term upside
data.description:
Berenberg said it continues to like the Antofagasta equity story and reckons that its "quality growth profile" was "compelling and one of the best, if not the best, in the sector".

"We also recognise the quality of its assets, as well as the helpful gold by-product credits that we think can act as an offset against rising cost inflation from oil and sulphuric acid prices," said Berenberg,

However, with Antofagasta's shares touching 4,000p following the release of its first quarter operational results, Berenberg said it had considered its valuation and the trajectory of the company's earnings over the next 12 months, and expect more of a sideways share price direction, before seeing further upside from the second half of 2027 as its core growth projects begin to deliver and production increases.

The German bank said it sees copper volume growth of roughly 6% from 2026-2027, while it also expects volume growth of around 16% for the 2027-2028 period.

"As such, we think that the shares will really start to kick on further when the 2028 volume growth is closer to delivery," said Berenberg, which added Antofagasta shares were currently trading at 2.87x net asset value and 9.3x 2026 underlying earnings.

Reporting by Iain Gilbert at Sharecast.com

data.title: AMD Stock Is on Its Best Run Since 2005. The Streak Can Keep Going. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Alex Kozul-Wright

While Nvidia's 11-day winning streak has been grabbing headlines lately, Advanced Micro Devices has been on a tear of its own.

Shares for the semiconductor company were up 0.4% in premarket trading Thursday. As of Wednesday's close, AMD stock was up 32% over 11 consecutive days — its longest closing streak since 2005 according to Dow Jones Market Data.

Shares are up 192% over the past 12 months, and 21% since the start of the year. More recently the stock has capitalized on broad market optimism, including for tech companies, relating to the recent cease-fire in Iran.

For its part, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing — the world's largest contract chip maker — reported a sharp rise in quarterly profit on Thursday. Other chip stocks were also rising ahead of the U.S. market open — Nvidia, Intel and Marvell were all higher.

The chip optimism means AMD and Nvidia's impressive streaks look set to keep going for now.

Write to Alex Kozul-Wright at alexander.kozul-wright@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: European Stocks Move Higher On U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Hopes
data.description:
BRUSSELS (dpa-AFX) - European stocks moved modestly higher on Thursday amid optimism the upcoming talks between officials from Iran and the U.S. will result in a potential resolution to the Middle East conflict.

According to reports, the warring nations are considering extending the ceasefire by two weeks in order to allow more time for talks.

Investors also digested corporate earnings updates and the latest batch of economic data from the region.

The pan European Stoxx 600 was up 0.15% at 618.22 a little while ago. The U.K.'s FTSE 100 advanced to 10,592.71, gaining 33.13 points or 0.31%. Germany's DAX was up 55.60 points or 0.23% at 24,143.02, while France's CAC 40 gained 29.43 points or 0.36% at 8,304.00

In the German market, Zalando moved up 3.2%. SAP gained about 2.3% and Brenntag climbed nearly 2%. Beiersdorf, MTU Aero Engines and Heidelberg Materials gained 1%-1.3%.

Qiagen, Merck, Deutsche Telekom, Mercedes-Benz, Daimler Truck Holding, BMW and Volkswagen lost 0.5%-1.6%.

In Paris, Dassault Systemes moved 2.2% up. Capgemini, Teleperformance, Saint-Gobain, Airbus, Publicis Groupe and Michelin gained 1.2%-2%.

Kering drifted down 1.7%. L'Oreal, ArcelorMittal, TotalEnergies and Engie also showed weakness.

In the UK market, Entain jumped 7.5%, thanks to the company maintaining its revenue outlook. Halma, B&M European Value Retail, Vistry Group, Frasers Group, JD Sports Fashion, Pershing Square Holdings, RightMove and Persimmon gained 2%-3.5%.

Tesco moved up sharply after the company reported strong sales and profit growth, and announced a 500 million pound buyback.

EasyJet dropped by about 5% amid uncertain situation in the Middle East. Airtel Africa, Convatec Group, Unite Group, Vodafone and Antofagasta lost 1.6%-2%.

In economic news, data from the Office for National Statistics showed UK's gross domestic product logged a monthly growth of 0.5% in February, outpacing the 0.1% expansion in January. Economists had forecast the growth to remain unchanged at 0.1%. Compared to the same period last year, GDP advanced 1% in February.

On the production-side, the dominant service sector expanded 0.5% and construction output advanced 1% in February.

Industrial production grew 0.5%, following successive falls of 0.1% in January and a 0.4% drop in December. Meanwhile, manufacturing output edged down 0.1%, reversing January's 0.2% increase.

On a yearly basis, industrial production slid 0.4% and manufacturing dropped 0.5% in February.

Eurozone inflation accelerated more than initially estimated in March, reaching its strongest level since mid-2024, final data from Eurostat showed.

The harmonized index of consumer prices rose 2.6% on a yearly basis in March. This was revised up from the initial estimate of 2.5% and followed a 1.9% rise in February.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: ZAWYA: Savoye appoints Hakim Ramadan to lead Middle East expansion amid accelerating logistics growth
data.description:
• Hakim Ramadan will lead Savoye Middle East’s regional operations and commercial development, while strengthening customer engagement and supporting the company’s growth across the region​​

Savoye, a leading integrator of automated warehouse solutions and software publisher in the Middle East, has appointed Hakim Ramadan as General Manager of Savoye Middle East. In this role, he will lead Savoye’s regional operations and commercial strategy, with a focus on scaling automation-led intralogistics solutions and deepening customer partnerships across high-growth sectors.

The appointment comes at a pivotal time, as the company continues to experience strong momentum in the region, driven by a rising demand for advanced logistics and supply chain solutions. The Middle East region’s logistics market is projected to grow at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 7.9 per cent through 2028, driven by increasing automation adoption, rapid eCommerce expansion, and significant regional infrastructure investments that are transforming supply chain efficiency.

Massimiliano Fochetti, Chief Sales Officer at Savoye, said: “The Middle East represents a key growth market for Savoye, and Hakim Ramadan’s appointment marks an important step in accelerating our expansion across the region. His proven track record in automation and intralogistics, combined with his deep understanding of regional market dynamics, will enable us to deliver greater value to our customers and further strengthen our leadership position.”

As General Manager of Savoye Middle East, Hakim Ramadan will focus on strengthening regional operations, supporting commercial development, and advancing tailored intralogistics solutions for customers navigating increasingly complex supply chain requirements. His appointment is expected to further reinforce Savoye’s regional leadership and customer responsiveness across the Middle East.

Hakim said: “I am pleased to join Savoye at an important time for the logistics and supply chain sector in the Middle East. The region continues to see growing demand for more agile, efficient and future-ready intralogistics operations, and I look forward to working closely with customers and partners to support that journey with practical, high-impact solutions.”

Hakim brings around three decades of experience in intralogistics, automation and business development. Prior to joining Savoye, he served as Director & Partner at Miebach Consulting in the UAE, where he focused on business development and subject matter expertise. Earlier in his career, he held senior leadership roles at Jungheinrich, including Managing Director of Jungheinrich Lift Truck Middle East and Regional Director for the Middle East & North Africa. He also served as Managing Director of BITO Storage Systems Middle East and Area Manager for the Middle East, North Africa and Turkey.

Since expanding into the Middle East market in 2021, Savoye has established a growing footprint in the region by combining automation, software and packaging to deliver tailored intralogistics solutions. With Hakim Ramadan’s appointment, the company is well-positioned to scale its presence, expand strategic partnerships, and play a leading role in shaping the future of automated intralogistics across the Middle East.

For further information, please contact:

CommuniGate Middle East

P.O.Box 500270, Dubai, UAE

E-mail: info@communigateme.com

Website: www.communigateme.com

Send us your press releases to pressrelease.zawya@lseg.com

data.title: Hooker Furnishings posts Q4 net income; FY revenue $278.1M, operating loss $16.5M
data.description:
Hooker Furnishings reported fourth-quarter fiscal 2026 net income of $0.5 million and full-year net loss of $27.0 million, reflecting a shorter fiscal year and divestitures. Revenue for the 52 weeks ended February 1, 2026 was $278.1 million, with gross profit of $73.5 million and an operating loss of $16.5 million driven primarily by non-cash impairment charges. The company completed the divestiture of Pulaski and Samuel Lawrence and implemented significant cost reductions and operational changes during the year.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $66.983 million for the 13 weeks ended February 1, 2026; $278.139 million for the 52 weeks ended February 1, 2026.
  • Gross profit: $20.097 million for the quarter; $73.495 million for the full year (gross margin 30.0% quarterly; 26.4% year).
  • Operating (loss) / income: $0.629 million operating income in Q4; $(16.464) million operating loss for the full year (includes $15.576 million goodwill and tradename impairment charges).
  • Net (loss) / income: $0.536 million net income for the quarter; $(26.967) million consolidated net loss for the full year.
  • Per-share results (basic/diluted): $0.05 basic and diluted earnings per share for the quarter; $(2.54) basic and diluted loss per share for the full year.

Business Highlights

  • Portfolio simplification: Completed divestiture of Pulaski Furniture and Samuel Lawrence Furniture, exiting lower-margin businesses and recording discontinued operations losses tied to those sales.
  • Cost and structural actions: Reduced fixed costs by about $26.3 million (approximately $17.5 million related to continuing operations) and recorded approximately $2.0 million in restructuring charges in continuing operations.
  • Segment performance: Hooker Branded returned to profitability with $1.9 million operating income for the year and a 200 basis-point full-year gross margin expansion; Domestic Upholstery showed significant gross margin improvement and reduced its Q4 operating loss by more than 50% year-over-year despite incurring large impairment charges.
  • Operational initiatives: Opened an Asia fulfillment warehouse and implemented disciplined pricing (5.7% average selling price increase in Hooker Branded) and freight cost reductions contributing to margin improvement.
  • Order momentum and backlog: Incoming orders were flat for Hooker Branded and increased sequentially for segments (orders up year-over-year for three consecutive quarters when adjusted), with consolidated backlog rising to $43.9 million at February 1, 2026 (up 19.7% from $36.6 million a year earlier).

Original SEC Filing: HOOKER FURNISHINGS Corp [ HOFT ] - 8-K - Apr. 16, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: CareDx to Sell CareDx AB and Kitted Lab Assets to Eurobio Scientific for $170 Million
data.description:
CareDx entered into a Purchase Agreement to sell its wholly owned Swedish subsidiary, CareDx AB, and certain kitted laboratory products and software assets to Eurobio Scientific for $170 million in cash, subject to working capital and other customary adjustments. Closing is subject to customary conditions, including regulatory approval, and the parties will enter into a transition services agreement at closing. The deal grants CareDx exclusive rights to distribute Eurobio’s NGS- and PCR-based post-transplant assays in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, while allowing Eurobio to continue serving its existing customers and to sell CareDx’s current chimerism assay to CareDx’s existing North American customers for three years.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Purchase Agreement to divest subsidiary and related assets, with distribution rights
  • Counterparty: Eurobio Scientific
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 15 2026 / Apr 15 2026
  • Duration / Termination: N/A
  • Reason: Refocus portfolio and monetize kitted lab products

Original SEC Filing: CareDx, Inc. [ CDNA ] - 8-K - Apr. 16, 2026

data.title: NEXTNRG 10-K: $81.8M Revenue, $(0.72) EPS on $88.2M Net Loss
data.description:
NEXTNRG reported fiscal-year net revenue of $81.835 million and a basic and diluted loss per share of $(0.72), reflecting a substantial year-over-year revenue increase driven by higher volumes and pricing but a steep overall net loss of $(88.176) million due to elevated operating expenses and investments in scale. Gross margin improved to $6.907 million from $1.787 million a year earlier, while operating loss widened, driven by higher depreciation and other scale-up costs. The company is emphasizing membership recurring revenue and expanded delivery capacity as it scales operations.

Financial Highlights

  • Sales – net: $81.835 million, up $54.065 million (194.69%) versus prior year per MD&A.
  • Gross margin: $6.907 million, improved from $1.787 million in the prior year.
  • Loss from operations: $(70.193) million (operating loss increased versus prior year).
  • Net loss: $(88.176) million, $(66.779) million worse year-over-year (312.10% change).
  • Basic and diluted loss per share: $(0.72); weighted average shares outstanding: 122.110 million.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers: Revenues surged approximately 195% year-over-year driven by higher gallons delivered and increased average price per gallon.
  • Channel & customer mix: Expansion into new regions and strengthened commercial fleet partnerships boosted recurring contracted deliveries.
  • Brand & technology: App enhancements and targeted marketing improved customer acquisition, scheduling, and order conversion.
  • Operational scale-up: Significant investment in delivery vehicles and capital assets expanded capacity and contributed to higher depreciation expense.
  • Membership model: Monthly memberships provide recurring revenue recognized over one-month cycles, supporting predictable short-term revenue streams.

Original SEC Filing: NEXTNRG, INC. [ NXXT ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Jocom Holdings 10-K: Revenue $0, Net loss $(839,842), EPS not disclosed
data.description:
Jocom Holdings reported zero revenue for the fiscal year, a steep decline from $24,000 in the prior year, and posted a net loss of $839,842 (basic and diluted EPS not numerically disclosed; weighted average shares 64,632,881). The operating loss of $937,220 was driven by increased general and administrative expenses, including a $648,000 impairment. The company is shifting strategy toward growth via business combinations and potential biotech opportunities.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 in 2025 vs $24,000 in 2024 — decline attributed to change of directors and direction.
  • Gross Profit: $0 in 2025 vs $24,000 in 2024.
  • Operating Income: Operating loss of $(937,220) in 2025; loss driven by G&A expenses and includes a $648,000 impairment.
  • Net Income: Net loss of $(839,842) in 2025 vs net profit of $68,519 in 2024.
  • Net Income Per Share: Basic and diluted per-share amounts not numerically disclosed; weighted average shares outstanding 64,632,881 for 2025.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Trend: Revenue fell to zero as the company shifted strategy following director changes.
  • Strategic Direction: Transitioning away from a data-analytics/grocery SaaS focus toward growth through business combinations and exploring potential acquisition in the biotech sector.
  • Operational Milestones: Entered a nondisclosure agreement for a China biotech target in June 2025 and began negotiations for an AI-driven insect-repellent collaboration.
  • Management & Restructuring: New CEO appointed August 22, 2025; restructuring and increased general and administrative activity prompted an impairment of receivables that affected operations.

Original SEC Filing: JOCOM HOLDINGS CORP. [ JOCM ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Eurozone Inflation for March Revised Higher on Sharper Energy Prices — Update
data.description:
By Ed Frankl

Inflation in the eurozone was a bit higher in March than previously thought, as the energy shock from the Iran war proved more pronounced than expected.

Consumer prices were up 2.6% on year last month, the highest level since July 2024 and up from 1.9% in February. It was also an upward revision from the provisional estimate of 2.5% published on March 31.

Energy inflation for March was revised up to 5.1% from the 4.9% previously reported. In February, energy was 3.1% cheaper than a year prior. Meanwhile, core inflation, which strips out more volatile energy and food costs, was unchanged from the flash reading at 2.3%.

The second estimate of inflation takes a more comprehensive overview of the data, including a higher coverage of subsectors used to calculate the overall figure.

"Inflation is headed for 3%, and it will stay close to this level for a while," Claus Vistesen, chief eurozone economist at Pantheon Macroeconomics, said in a note to clients.

While the impact of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz on inflation is already evident, much about future effects will depend on the duration and extent of the war. While central banks sometimes see pronounced increases in energy costs as temporary, they can grow more concerned if price pressures spread to more persistent forms of second-round inflation via, for instance,spiraling wage costs.

"A significant inflation shock is still on the way, even if we assume relatively modest second-round effects in food and core inflation," Vistesen said.

European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde said this week that the impact of the war was landing between the bank's baseline and adverse scenarios, the latter of which predicts a sharper rise in inflation and slower economic growth.

"We have to be completely agile and ready to move in the direction that is required," she said on the sidelines of an International Monetary Fund meeting in Washington, D.C.

The bank, which said in March that it expected inflation to average 2.6% this year, next meets on April 30. Investors have ramped up bets on interest-rate rises since the first strikes on Iran in late February. They have priced in at least two quarter-point hikes this year, according to LSEG data.

The IMF on Tuesday downgraded its forecast for eurozone growth this year to 1.1% from the 1.3% growth it forecast in January.

Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com

data.title: Domo 10-K: $318.9M Revenue, $(1.45) EPS
data.description:
Domo reported fiscal 2026 revenue of $318.9 million and a net loss per share of $(1.45), with the company narrowing its net loss to $(59.3) million from $(81.9) million a year earlier. Revenue was essentially flat year-over-year driven by steady subscription revenue, while improved operating efficiency reduced the loss from operations. Domo continues to emphasize consumption-based pricing and product investment to drive longer-term ARR stability.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $318.9 million (≈1% YoY increase from $317.0 million)
  • Gross profit: $239.1 million; gross margin 75%
  • Loss from operations: $(39.1) million (improved from $(59.3) million prior year)
  • Net loss: $(59.3) million (improved from $(81.9) million prior year)
  • Net loss per share: $(1.45); weighted-average shares 40.984 million
  • Accumulated deficit: $1,546.9 million as of January 31, 2026

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix: Subscription revenue comprised approximately 91% of total revenue and remained roughly flat at ~$289 million.
  • Pricing model shift: Significant transition to consumption-based pricing with 84% of ARR on a consumption model to increase predictability.
  • Customer base & retention: Over 2,400 customers; ARR net retention improved to 96% by Q4 2026, with enterprise revenue declining while corporate revenue increased.
  • Product and R&D: More than $1.006 billion invested in the platform since inception; ongoing investments in AI/ML and Appstore capabilities with R&D headcount at 263.
  • Operational efficiency: Management reduced sales & marketing and R&D spend as a percentage of revenue and focused on sales productivity and expanding the partner ecosystem.

Original SEC Filing: DOMO, INC. [ DOMO ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Data443 Risk Mitigation 10-K: $4.42M Revenue, $(2.57)M Net Loss
data.description:
Data443 Risk Mitigation reported $4.421 million in revenue for the fiscal year, a 9% decline from the prior year driven by one-time catch-up payments in the prior period, and posted a net loss of $(2.568) million that benefited from settlement gains. Gross profit was $2.286 million with a 52% margin, and operating loss totaled $(2.131) million. The results reflect ongoing shifts in go-to-market strategy and integration of acquired products as the company focuses on recurring revenue and cost efficiencies.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $4.421 million, decrease of $0.451 million (−9%) versus 2024; decline driven by one-time catch-up payments in prior year.
  • Gross Profit: $2.286 million; gross margin 52% (versus 58% in 2024).
  • Operating Income: Operating loss of $(2.131) million (operating loss of $2,130,967 as stated).
  • Net Income: Net loss of $(2.568) million, improvement of $3.519 million (−58%) versus 2024, reflecting settlement gains.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Trend: Reported revenue declined ~9% year-over-year due to prior-year catch-up payments, while annual recurring revenue (ARR) shows organic consumption growth.
  • Go-to-Market: Continued shift to channel-led and inbound sales with emphasis on renewals, customer success, and re-engaging customers from acquired products.
  • Product & Marketplace Momentum: Expanded product suite to include threat intelligence, privacy, ransomware recovery, TacitRed, and Vaikora, and onboarded offerings to Azure and AWS marketplaces to increase visibility.
  • Operational Efficiency: Integrated multiple acquisitions, consolidated data center costs, reduced consulting spend, and centralized cloud-native deployments to improve operational efficiency.

Original SEC Filing: Data443 Risk Mitigation, Inc. [ ATDS ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: China Foods Holdings 10-K: Revenue $0.33M, EPS $(0.02)
data.description:
China Foods Holdings reported fiscal-year revenue of $327,871, a 40.5% increase from the prior year, while basic and diluted net loss per share remained $(0.02). The company posted a net loss of $(0.399) million, an improvement from the prior-year loss of $(0.456) million, with operating losses narrowing alongside reduced operating expenses.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $327,871 (increase of 40.5% vs prior year; prior year $233,339)
  • Gross Profit: $29,962 (decreased from $53,873; decline attributed to lower wine sales)
  • Operating Income: Loss from operations $(0.399) million; operating expenses decreased (G&A down from $502,654 to $423,644)
  • Net Income: Net loss $(0.399) million, improved from prior-year net loss of $(0.456) million
  • Net Income Per Share: Basic and diluted $(0.02), unchanged from prior year

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth Drivers: Higher demand in wine and healthcare product sales supported the 40.5% revenue increase year-over-year.
  • Channel Expansion: Expanded online presence through Tmall, WeChat, TikTok and Xiaohongshu while continuing distributor-led offline sales.
  • Product and Brand Momentum: Broadened product mix to include supplements, meal replacements, skincare and high-end wine; launched nutrition consulting via social media channels.
  • Operational Efficiency: Focused C2M (consumer-to-manufacturer) supply-chain collaboration to reduce logistics and inventory costs and reduced G&A expenses, notably rent.

Original SEC Filing: China Foods Holdings Ltd. [ CFOO ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Catalyst Crew Technologies 2025 10-K: $0 Revenue, $(0.01) EPS
data.description:
Catalyst Crew Technologies reported no revenue for the years ended December 31, 2025 and 2024 and posted a net loss of $207,485 in 2025, or $(0.01) per share, driven by operating expenses that produced an operating loss of $171,852. The company reported an improved net loss versus $3,261,038 in 2024 but continues to have no commercialization revenue. Management cites substantial doubt about the company's ability to continue as a going concern and plans to prioritize fundraising and licensing to resume operations.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 for the years ended December 31, 2025 and 2024.
  • Operating Income: $(171,852) loss from operations for the year ended December 31, 2025.
  • Net Income: $(207,485) net loss for the year ended December 31, 2025 (improved from $(3,261,038) in 2024).
  • Net Income Per Share: $(0.01) net loss per common share — basic and diluted for the year ended December 31, 2025.

Business Highlights

  • Commercialization Status: No revenue recognized in 2025 or 2024, indicating no near-term sales traction or commercialization to date.
  • Operational Cost Drivers: Operating expenses increased notably in 2024 due in part to higher stock-based compensation, reflecting investment in personnel and retention efforts.
  • Strategic Direction: Management intends to pursue licensing agreements and seek private equity or debt financing to support operations and future commercialization efforts.
  • Going Concern and Priorities: The company disclosed substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern and will prioritize fundraising and product licensing to attempt to resume and sustain operations.

Original SEC Filing: Catalyst Crew Technologies Corp. [ CCTC ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.
